notes:
Hôm qua means yesterday — the day before today. It talks about something finished in the past.

It usually goes at the beginning or end of a sentence. It often appears with past markers like đã, but that’s optional if the time is clear.

Related words: hôm nay (today), ngày mai (tomorrow). You might also hear ngày hôm qua, which is slightly more formal but means the same thing.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I met an old friend at the coffee shop yesterday.
I bought a new shirt at the mall yesterday.
I forgot to do my homework yesterday so I have to turn it in late today.

content.sentence: Chúng ta sẽ đi chơi ___ nhé.

exampleSentences: Anh đã gặp bạn cũ ở quán cà phê hôm qua.
Chị mua một chiếc áo mới ở trung tâm thương mại hôm qua.
Em quên làm bài tập về nhà hôm qua nên hôm nay phải nộp muộn.
notes_md:
**Hôm qua** means **yesterday** — the day before today. It talks about something finished in the past.

It usually goes at the **beginning or end of a sentence**. It often appears with past markers like **đã**, but that’s optional if the time is clear.


Related words: **hôm nay** (today), **ngày mai** (tomorrow). You might also hear **ngày hôm qua**, which is slightly more formal but means the same thing.

example.en: I went to the movies yesterday.
example.vi: Hôm qua tôi đã đi xem phim.
